edges.description: Tooth and oral-surface phenotypes reflect disrupted WNT10A-dependent ectodermal placode and odontogenic development.
edges.target: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

edges.description: Hair and nail phenotypes reflect impaired appendage morphogenesis downstream of WNT10A signaling loss.
Palmoplantar keratinization and sweating phenotypes reflect epidermal and sweat-gland involvement.
edges.target: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

edges.description: In the SSPS end of the WNT10A spectrum, adnexal involvement produces eyelid hidrocystomas and skin-neoplasm risk.
edges.target: Eyelid Adnexal Cyst and Skin Tumor Susceptibility

edges.source: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

edges.description: Milder WNT10A dosage effects manifest as selective tooth agenesis.
edges.source: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

edges.description: Disrupted tooth development can alter tooth morphology.
edges.source: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

edges.description: Oral ectodermal involvement reduces tongue papillae.
edges.source: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

edges.description: Nail-unit dysplasia manifests as nail dystrophy.
Hair appendage involvement manifests as hypotrichosis or sparse hair.
Hair appendage involvement can manifest as dry hair texture.
Palmoplantar epidermal dysfunction manifests as keratoderma.
edges.source: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

edges.description: Sweat-apparatus involvement manifests as palmoplantar hyperhidrosis.
edges.source: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

edges.description: Epidermal dysfunction manifests as dry skin.
edges.source: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

edges.description: Epidermal differentiation abnormalities manifest as hyperkeratosis.
edges.source: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

edges.description: Eyelid adnexal cyst susceptibility manifests as apocrine hidrocystomas.
edges.source: Eyelid Adnexal Cyst and Skin Tumor Susceptibility

edges.description: Adnexal tumor susceptibility manifests as increased skin neoplasm risk.
edges.source: Eyelid Adnexal Cyst and Skin Tumor Susceptibility

nodes.description: Teeth that are present may show morphological abnormalities including microdontia, peg-shaped teeth, or enamel defects.

evidence.snippet: Anomalies in tooth morphology were frequently observed with heterozygous patients displaying hypodontia.

nodes.description: WNT10A deficiency disrupts tooth and oral ectodermal placode development, producing severe oligodontia, selective tooth agenesis, tooth-shape anomalies, and reduced tongue papillae.
nodes.id: Dental and Oral Ectodermal Placode Defects

evidence.snippet: Mild to severe oligodontia was observed in all patients bearing biallelic WNT10A mutations.

nodes.description: Dry scalp hair is part of the classic OODD ectodermal appendage phenotype, distinct from reduced hair density.

evidence.snippet: Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia is a rare autosomal recessive syndrome in which the presenting phenotype is dry hair, severe hypodontia, smooth tongue with marked reduction of fungiform and filiform papillae, onychodysplasia, keratoderma and hyperhidrosis of palms and soles, and hyperkeratosis of the skin.

nodes.description: Generalized dry skin (xerosis) reflecting ectodermal involvement beyond palmoplantar surfaces.

evidence.snippet: severe oligodontia, onychodysplasia, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, dry skin, hypotrichosis, and hyperhidrosis of the palms and soles

nodes.description: Disrupted WNT/beta-catenin signaling during embryonic development leads to hypoplasia or aplasia of ectodermal appendages. Teeth are most severely affected (oligodontia/anodontia), followed by nails (dystrophy, thinning), hair (sparse, dry), and sweat glands (hypohidrosis or paradoxical hyperhidrosis of palms/soles).

evidence.snippet: Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia (OODD) is a rare form of ectodermal dysplasia characterized by severe oligodontia, onychodysplasia, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, dry skin, hypotrichosis, and hyperhidrosis of the palms and soles.

nodes.description: The Schopf-Schulz-Passarge end of the WNT10A spectrum includes eyelid apocrine hidrocystomas and increased benign or malignant skin-tumor risk, indicating adnexal-tumor susceptibility beyond the classic OODD appendage phenotype.
nodes.id: Eyelid Adnexal Cyst and Skin Tumor Susceptibility

evidence.snippet: Schöpf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome, which is so far considered a unique entity by the findings of numerous cysts along eyelid margins and the increased risk of benign and malignant skin tumors

nodes.description: Multiple apocrine hidrocystomas of the eyelids, distinguishing Schopf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome from classic OODD.

evidence.snippet: Schöpf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome, which is so far considered a unique entity by the findings of numerous cysts along eyelid margins and the increased risk of benign and malignant skin tumors
Schöpf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome (SSPS; MIM224750) is a rare autosomal recessive form of ectodermal dysplasia that was recently shown to result from mutations in the WNT10A gene.

nodes.description: Disrupted ectodermal appendage morphogenesis affects hair shafts and nail units, producing dry or sparse hair and nail dystrophy.

evidence.snippet: severe oligodontia, onychodysplasia, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, dry skin, hypotrichosis, and hyperhidrosis of the palms and soles

nodes.description: Generalized hyperkeratosis of the skin beyond palmoplantar surfaces.

evidence.snippet: hyperkeratosis of the skin

nodes.description: Nail abnormalities including thinning, ridging, brittleness, and slow growth affecting fingernails and toenails.

evidence.snippet: a rare syndrome characterised by severe hypodontia, nail dystrophy, smooth tongue, dry skin, keratoderma and hyperhydrosis of palms and soles

nodes.description: Absence of six or more permanent teeth is the hallmark feature, present in virtually all homozygous/compound heterozygous patients. Commonly affects mandibular second premolars, maxillary lateral incisors, and lower incisors.

evidence.snippet: Mild to severe oligodontia was observed in all patients bearing biallelic WNT10A mutations.
Patients with bi-allelic WNT10A mutations have severe tooth agenesis while heterozygous individuals are either unaffected or have a mild phenotype.

nodes.description: WNT10A-related epidermal and sweat-apparatus defects produce palmoplantar keratoderma, broader hyperkeratosis or xerosis, and abnormal sweating of palms and soles.
nodes.id: Palmoplantar Epidermal and Sweat Gland Dysfunction

evidence.snippet: severe oligodontia, onychodysplasia, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, dry skin, hypotrichosis, and hyperhidrosis of the palms and soles

nodes.description: Excessive sweating localized to palms and soles, paradoxically co-occurring with dry skin elsewhere.

evidence.snippet: keratoderma and hyperhidrosis of palms and soles

nodes.description: Thickened, hyperkeratotic skin on palms and soles, often with a smooth waxy texture. A cardinal feature of OODD and SSPS.

evidence.snippet: Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia (OODD), a rare autosomal-recessive inherited form of ectodermal dysplasia including severe oligodontia, nail dystrophy, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, and hyperhidrosis

nodes.description: Congenital absence of one or more teeth, the mildest dental phenotype seen especially in heterozygous carriers of WNT10A mutations.

evidence.snippet: The authors identified WNT10A as a major gene in the aetiology of isolated hypodontia. By including WNT10A in the DNA diagnostics of isolated tooth agenesis, the yield of molecular testing in this condition was significantly increased from 15% to 71%.

nodes.description: Increased risk of benign and malignant skin tumors, a distinguishing feature of Schopf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome within the WNT10A spectrum.

evidence.snippet: Schöpf-Schulz-Passarge syndrome, which is so far considered a unique entity by the findings of numerous cysts along eyelid margins and the increased risk of benign and malignant skin tumors

nodes.description: Smooth tongue with marked reduction of fungiform and filiform papillae, reflecting ectodermal involvement in tongue surface differentiation.

evidence.snippet: smooth tongue with marked reduction of fungiform and filiform papillae

nodes.description: Thin, dry, and sparse scalp hair. Body hair may also be reduced.

evidence.snippet: dry hair
hypotrichosis

nodes.description: WNT10A encodes a secreted glycoprotein ligand of the canonical WNT signaling pathway. Loss-of-function mutations abolish WNT10A binding to Frizzled receptors, preventing activation of the beta-catenin destruction complex inhibition and downstream TCF/LEF-mediated transcription. This disrupts ectodermal appendage morphogenesis including teeth, hair follicles, nails, and sweat glands.

evidence.snippet: This is the first report to our knowledge of a human phenotype resulting from a mutation in WNT10A, and it is the first demonstration of an ectodermal dysplasia caused by an altered WNT signaling pathway, expanding the list of WNT-related diseases.
WNT10A is expressed in the skin and epidermis and it has shown to be critical for the development of ectodermal appendages.

WNT10A gene encodes a canonical wingless pathway signaling molecule involved in cell fate specification as well as morphogenetic patterning of the developing ectoderm, nervous system, skeleton, and tooth.
Screening of candidate genes in this region led us to identify the same c.697G-->T (p.Glu233X) homozygous nonsense mutation in exon 3 of the WNT10A gene in all patients.
about half of the heterozygotes (53.8%) show a phenotype manifestation, including mainly tooth and nail anomalies, which was not reported before in OODD

heterozygotes show a sex-biased manifestation pattern, with a significantly higher proportion of tooth anomalies in males than in females, which may implicate gender-specific differences of WNT10A expression

Mutations in the WNT10A gene were first detected in the rare syndrome odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia (OODD, OMIM257980) but have now also been found to cause about 35-50% of selective tooth agenesis (STHAG4, OMIM150400)

nodes.description: Nonsense mutation reported in both OODD and SSPS, illustrating allelic heterogeneity and phenotypic variability with the same mutation.

evidence.snippet: a 59-year-old woman with SSPS in whom a homozygous nonsense mutation (p.Cys107X) in WNT10A was detected

nodes.description: Missense mutation in a conserved alpha-helix domain of WNT10A. Identified in a consanguineous Pakistani family with complete OODD.

evidence.snippet: a homozygous c.392C>T transition in exon 3 of WNT10A, which predicts a p.A131V substitution in a conserved alpha-helix domain

nodes.description: Most common pathogenic WNT10A allele in European populations. Allele frequency approximately 10 times higher in tooth agenesis cohorts than in general population databases.

evidence.snippet: The WNT10A Phe228Ile variant alone reached an allele frequency of 0.21 in the tooth agenesis cohort, about 10 times higher than the allele frequency reported in large SNP databases for Caucasian populations.

nodes.description: First identified pathogenic WNT10A variant. Homozygous nonsense mutation found in consanguineous Lebanese families with OODD. Predicted to cause premature truncation at 232 of 417 amino acids.

evidence.snippet: Screening of candidate genes in this region led us to identify the same c.697G-->T (p.Glu233X) homozygous nonsense mutation in exon 3 of the WNT10A gene in all patients.
